'''Puullo''' was a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] criminal who served as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] with fellow Hutt [[Shettora]]'s [[Shettora's criminal faction|criminal faction]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]]. He effectively controlled the [[Black market/Legends|black market]] on Brentaal IV and was fair in his [[Merchant/Legends|dealings]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the faction that Puullo was a part of made a deal with the crew of the [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]'', which gave the organization a share of the profits the ''Far Orbit'' captured in exchange for a [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] from which to operate and other illegal goods and services.

==Biography==
Puullo was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] criminal who served as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] in the [[Shettora's criminal faction|criminal faction]] run by fellow Hutt [[Shettora]]. Puullo served at the forefront of the organization<ref name="Orbit" /> by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]<ref name="Date">{{FarOrbitDate}}</ref> and almost completely controlled the [[Black market/Legends|black market]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]]—where Shettora's faction was based—because of his expertise in [[Merchant/Legends|business]]. The Hutt was also involved with several other [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] criminal factions. Puullo was well-liked by individuals in the underworld and was fair in the dealings he made. During the Hutt's career as a criminal, a few individuals who tried to take advantage of him were never heard of again. Puullo, when traveling, was [[Bodyguard/Legends|protected]] by a group of criminals who owed the Hutt a favor. The Hutt also specialized in procuring [[Starship/Legends|starships]], and he only dealt in quality merchandise.<ref name="Orbit" />

{{RPGseed}}
In 0 ABY, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Dhas Fenoep Vedij]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]-aligned [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]'' contacted the Hutts on Brentaal IV. The Hutts dealt with Vedij, and an agreement was reached; for 30 percent of the goods and [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] the ''Far Orbit'' seized—excluding military hardware—the Hutts would grant them a hidden [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]—known as a [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]]—to use, illegal weapons, and protection from any [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] seeking the [[Frigate/Legends|frigate's]] crew.<ref name="Orbit" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Unlike most other Hutts, Puullo was, for the most part, well-liked by the individuals in the criminal underworld. He was soft spoken and was fair and even-handed in his negotiations, though he could be a bit uncompromising. Possessing a pleasant demeanor, the Hutt was patient and avoided violence when he could, though some individuals who tried to take advantage of him, after mistaking the Hutt as weak, were never seen again. Puullo was shrewd in nature and was quite good at dealing with businesses, which allowed him to practically control the black market on Brentaal IV. The Hutt also refused to deal in defective goods, instead preferring quality merchandise.<ref name="Orbit" />

He took responsibility for his goods, and if an item that he sold malfunctioned, Puullo sometimes would refund the customer. Puullo was competent in the many skills sometimes required in the underworld, from forgery to operating a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicle. The Hutt was also somewhat immune to the [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricks]] that could be used by those strong in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="Orbit" />

==Equipment==
Puullo wore a vest with many pockets and owned a concealed [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] transit pass, as well as an encrypted [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]].<ref name="Orbit" />

==Behind the scenes==
Puullo was first mentioned in [[1998]]'s ''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'', by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]]. The book was a supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and had the same theme of the previous publication, ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'', as it followed the privateering vessel ''Far Orbit''. In the scenario hook known as ''The Hutt Gambit'', players may decide to get into contact with the Hutts to acquire extra supplies and services for a price; in the dealings that follow, although it is never actually stated, the book infers that Puullo takes part and is later the main character to whom the crew of the ''Far Orbit'' speak whenever they trade goods. Puullo and the Hutts may require the characters to pass a test before they sign the deal. If the crew of the ''Far Orbit'' raid a [[Xizor Transport Systems/Legends|Xizor Transport Systems]]' [[Transport/Legends|transport]], Puullo will back out of the deal with the frigate after declaring the agreement void.<ref name="Orbit" />
